sglang.title: Fix edge case for truncated
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

follow up #1739

When len(req.origin_input_ids) == self.max_req_input_len, the input is actually not truncated. We shouldn't show the warning message

Modifications

Fix the condition

 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.

sglang.title: Fuse more ops & Simplify token mapping
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Use torch.compile to fuse more operators
Simplify token mapping
Skip softmax for greedy sampling

This should bring 1% - 2% improvement.

vllm.author: chenqianfzh
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR completes TP in Qwen2 bnb, which was recently added in #9467.
It is verified using Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B with TP_size = 1 ,2 ,4.

vllm.title: [Frontend] Support custom request_id from request
vllm.author: guoyuhong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FILL IN THE PR DESCRIPTION HERE:
Currently, request_id is generated in function create_chat_completion, which cannot be controlled by high-level user.
In our scenario, we want to pass the custom request_id into create_chat_completion. Then, we can debug the end-to-end process  using a unique request

vllm.title: [BugFix] Prevent exporting duplicate OpenTelemetry spans
vllm.author: ronensc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
After merging #7049, I noticed that non-beam search requests result in duplicate OpenTelemetry spans being exported.
This PR resolves the issue by passing the finished_before list to do_tracing(), similar to its use in do_log_stats().
cc @megha95

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Generate exactly input_len tokens in benchmark_throughput
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
benchmark_throughput.py will generate a sequence by prompt = "hi" * (args.input_len - 1) when using --input-len. This sequence is like "hihihihihihi" and the tokenizer cannot generate args.input_len token_ids.
This pr do the following fixes:

change the sequence to "hi hi hi hi hi" to be friendly to

vllm.title: [Model] Support E5-V
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add support for E5-V, another multimodal embedding model, which uses the same model architecture as LLaVA-NeXT.
Also, I just realized that the embedding tests currently only input text into the model. I have fixed them so that image input is also being tested.

vllm.title: [Build] Fix `FetchContent` multiple build issue
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, all FetchContent dependencies live in .deps, and that includes their binary directories. During development with multiple builds, those different builds all place their binary dir for the dependency in .deps, leading to conflict.
This PR fixes this by overriding the dependency's BINARY_DI
